description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked wooden logs, featuring a steep gable roof with visible wood grain and weathering. The front facade includes a single wooden door with a small rectangular window above it, flanked by two log columns. Adjacent to the cabin, there is a stone-paved area with a simple campfire ring made of stones and several wooden stumps arranged as seats. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing minimal overhang. The cabin has a single, narrow window on each of the longer sides, and the overall structure is compact and functional, evoking a traditional wilderness retreat. The exterior is dominated by natural wood tones, with the stone area providing a contrasting texture.
description: A compact log cabin with a steep gable roof, wooden door, and small side windows, set beside a stone-paved area with a campfire and log seats. The structure features rustic wood textures and a simple, functional design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a wooden door, a small window above it, and the adjacent stone-paved area with a campfire ring and log seats.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the long wooden wall with a single narrow window and the full expanse of the gable roof, as well as the stone-paved area extending from the cabin.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the plain back wall of the cabin with no visible windows or doors, and the roof's peak running lengthwise.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the opposite long wall with another narrow window, the stone-paved area, and the campfire ring with log seats.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with steep pitch, constructed from wooden planks with visible weathering and slight overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: Two narrow, vertically oriented rectangular windows, one on each long side of the cabin, symmetrically placed near the center of each wall
attribute_door_placement: Single wooden door centered on the front facade beneath the gable, directly below a small window
